tooling_feedback.change: Instruction lookup now prefers getInstructionAt(addr) before getInstructionContaining(addr), which keeps exact-address probes anchored on the intended instruction boundary instead of occasionally snapping to an unrelated containing region.

validation.observation: The exact-address probe for 0x140020522 now starts at 0x140020522 and cleanly reaches 0x140020540..0x14002065E.

caller_window.role: Exact JS5/bootstrap publisher staging window immediately before wrapper 0x14002EC80.

evidence.observation: Stores addresses of the global JS5 modulus slot 0x140E58C18 and shared exponent slot 0x140E58C10 into [rbp -0x50] and [rbp -0x48].
Loads qword [rsi +0x19E90] into [rbp -0x40] and calls 0x14015D330 with output buffer [rbp +0x2B0].
Normalizes the 0x14015D330 result into a direct text pointer at [rbp -0x38].
Builds the second text field with format literal 0x140B69720 and stores the selected result pointer at [rbp -0x30].
Loads R15 from qword [0x140C57478] before the wrapper call.

Passes revision 947 in RDX, a string-like/context object at [rbp -0x70] in R8, the second text pointer wrapper at [rbp -0x30] in R9, and stack arguments that include [rbp -0x38], [rbp -0x40], [rbp +0x40], [rbp +0x430], global R15, [rbp +0x428], [rbp -0x48], [rbp -0x50], and [rbp -0x58].

wrapper_window.role: Normalizes the caller locals into concrete text clones, key objects, and scalar payloads before constructor 0x14058EE40, then publishes the local text clones through a shared callback path.

evidence.observation: Dereferences caller arg13 and clones that text into wrapper-local string object [rsp +0xA0].
Dereferences caller args 12 and 11 into concrete objects R14 and R15, which remain the JS5 modulus and shared exponent respectively.
Dereferences caller arg6 and overwrites wrapper slot [rsp +0x150] with the qword originally sourced from [client +0x19E90].
Dereferences caller arg5 and clones the normalized 0x14015D330 text result into wrapper-local string object [rsp +0x88].
Clones wrapper incoming R9 into wrapper-local string object [rsp +0x70], matching the second %s:%u text field staged by the caller.

Calls constructor 0x14058EE40 with revision dword [rsi], register R8 = wrapper incoming R8, register R9 = local clone [rsp +0x70], arg6 = dereferenced [client +0x19E90] qword, arg9 = caller global R15 from 0x140C57478, arg11 = shared exponent, and arg12 = JS5 modulus.

After construction, three repeated callback blocks consume wrapper-local clones [rsp +0x70], [rsp +0x88], and [rsp +0xA0] through the shared object at 0x140E57B40 rather than storing those clones directly into constructor field +0x48.

field_map.meaning: Copy of wrapper register arg R8, which is the caller's string-like/context object at [rbp -0x70].
Caller global/config qword loaded from [0x140C57478] into caller R15, then forwarded as constructor arg9 through wrapper slot [rsp +0x40]. This field is not the first formatter-backed text clone.
Shared exponent object sourced from global slot 0x140E58C10.
JS5 modulus object sourced from global slot 0x140E58C18.
Refcounted pair installed after the later helper chain rooted at 0x14058F122 rather than a direct raw copy of the wrapper-local string clones.

corrected_conclusions: The earlier model that treated constructor field +0x48 as the first formatter-backed endpoint string was wrong. The exact caller/wrapper/constructor handoff proves +0x48 is a separate global/config qword forwarded from 0x140C57478.

Wrapper 0x14002EC80 handles three distinct text clones: the selected precursor string, the normalized 0x14015D330 result, and the second %s:%u string. Those clones survive long enough to be published after construction through the 0x140E57B40 callback path.

The persistent constructor fields that are now proven with high confidence are +0x30 for the wrapper R8 context object, +0x48 for the global/config qword, +0x60 for the shared exponent object, and +0x68 for the JS5 modulus object.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: Corrects the medium-confidence +0x48 inference in win64c-js5-bootstrap-endpoint-publisher-and-slot-a-getter: the first formatter-backed text is still important, but it is not what lands in constructor field +0x48.
Strengthens the JS5 bootstrap model by turning the wrapper call into an exact argument-normalization map instead of a loose 'keys plus two strings' handoff description.
Adds a concrete tooling note: the workspace-local headless dump script now handles exact-address probes more safely for future bootstrap and JS5 mapping passes.
next_targets: Recover the exact semantic role of the caller's [rbp -0x70] object that becomes constructor field +0x30.
Name the qword loaded from [0x140C57478] now that it is proven to land in constructor field +0x48.
Follow the post-construction 0x140E57B40 callback path far enough to classify what the three wrapper-local text clones actually publish into the broader bootstrap state.
